rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY SINGER VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 22, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Hearing For Writ Of Mandate The hearing on Anthony Singer's petition for a writ of mandate is CONTINUED to June 14, 2019. The police department filed an opposition on May 9 stating, among other things, that it released redacted versions of the documents Singer sought on March 29, and Singer's petition is therefore moot. Singer shall file any response to the City's opposition on or before June 7, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: SFBVC LLC DBA THE BUENA VISTA CAFE VS. BLAZING SADDLES, LLC DBA BLAZING SADDLES BIKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Barrows Motion And Declaration Of Ramsey Kubein
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 22, 2019, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT W.C. DRILLING INC. DBA WEST COAST DRILLING's Motion To Quash Barrows Motion And Declaration Of Ramsey Kubein Cross-defendant W.C. Drilling, Inc.'s motion to quash/Barrows motion is DENIED. Cross-complainant Partners Contract, Inc. has a reasonable excuse for delay - it learned only in discovery of W.C. Drilling's role in causing the alleged damage. (See CCP §474; McOwen v. Grossman (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 937, 942.) Moreover, any prejudice is limited; W.C. has known it would be sued since at least January, which was nine months before the currently pending trial date. Motions for trial continuance are heard only in Department 206. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: DARREL REED VS. FRED LUXFORD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 22, 2019, Line 1 DEFENDANT NOR-CAL SCAFFOLDING, INC., FRED LUXFORD MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Nor-Cal Scaffolding, Inc. and Fred Luxford's unopposed Motion to Compel Further Responses to RFAs and to Compel Deposition with Production of Documents is GRANTED. Plaintiff is ordered to provide verified, code-compliant amended responses to all RFAs within ten days of Notice of this Order. Plaintiff shall make himself available for deposition and provide responsive documents within two weeks of Notice of this Order. If plaintiff fails to timely provide the amended responses or fails to make himself available for deposition, sanctions in the amount of $2,000 shall be due and payable against plaintiff in favor of defendants within three weeks of Notice of this Order. Notice of this Order shall be provided by overnight mail. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HAIQING QIU VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL ET AL
